forecasts.reasoning: To resolve NO, the word "tariff" (or "tariffs") must appear on the specified URLs for AT LEAST ONE of the two newspapers on EVERY SINGLE DAY for 42 consecutive days (May 20 to June 30). These URLs represent the print editions' front pages (NYT todayspaper header and WSJ frontpage), meaning the word must appear in a headline or short snippet, not just buried in full article text. Furthermore, WSJ does not publish a print edition on Sundays, removing it from the equation entirely for 6 days of the period. The burden then falls solely on the NYT to carry the streak on all 6 Sundays. Even if tariffs are a major news topic, the probability of daily continuous headline coverage over 6 uninterrupted weeks without a single day's break—especially through slow news days, Memorial Day, and the busy June SCOTUS decision cycle—is vanishingly small. The exponential decay of probabilities for maintaining such an unbroken streak makes a YES resolution nearly a statistical lock.
